body.u: Plan: Left school 16. Job. Apprentice 21 Finish apprenticeship, get job off the island. Get married, raise family. Get morgamortgage. Buy own home.

I left the Sheppey School in the summer of 1978. I found this school more boring every year. I did not agree with some of the teachers attitudes. It was hard finding a job, I failed a few chances, but eventually got what I wanted locally, a craft apprenticeship. I done a four year apprenticeship, the money was not spectacular, but it was worth it in the long run.

When I finished my apprenticeship I got a job away from home. The money was a lot better and I was able to get my own home. My craze on motorcycling never wore off, and I often was off for weekends on my machine. When I was in my mid-twentystwenties I started tooto settle down and get married. My wife and I decided not tooto have children immediately, we had a good time while we could, before we were tied down with our children. I was always worried about my children, educational wise. I wanted them tooto have a better education than I had. My wife is quite bright and sensible, and has a smashing personality. She also likes a good ride.

I am just coming up for retirement now. I am looking forward tooto retiring, I will be able tooto spend more time with my children and grandchildren. I have stayed with the same firm all my life. I think when I die I will want tooto be crimatedcremated. I have enjoyed my life. I have lived it how I wanted too. I have had a few worries in life but I have enjoyed it. I hope my children or grandchildren don’t put me in a home.
